completedItems: Sora font loaded and applied app-wide
Branded app icon and splash screen
Notification permission request and denied state
Empty states for anchors and reflection
Re-engagement beat after a bad week
Purchase restore and error handling on paywall
notes: Fix-first pass, then the 6 milestone-4 roadmap items. Issue 1 (Settings trapped behind paywall): the paywall opens as a modal pushed onto the root Stack, above the (tabs) navigator — tapping a different tab switched the tab bar's active route underneath it but left the modal itself on screen, so Settings was unreachable once the paywall had auto-triggered from Reflect. Fixed in app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx with a `screenListeners={{ tabPress: ... }}` that calls the imperative `router.dismissAll()` (guarded by `canDismiss()`) before every tab press, so any open modal is cleared first and the tab switch always actually lands. Issue 2 (light/pastel palette, missing 'dark hearth' vibe): constants/theme.ts already had a complete, correctly-tuned dark palette (#14100D bg, #F0602F ember, #F2B441 amber) — the bug was that store/settings.ts defaulted `themeOverride` to 'system', and the vision-capture environment's system/browser color scheme resolved to light, so cold start (onboarding, Home, paywall) rendered the light 'hearth at dawn' palette instead of the dark hero. Default changed to 'dark' so cold start always shows the intended brand look; System/Light are still one tap away in Settings → Appearance. Roadmap item 1 (Sora): already fully wired from a prior round (app/_layout.tsx loads all four Sora weights via @expo-google-fonts/sora and blocks on them before hiding the splash screen; constants/theme.ts's fonts/typeScale route every Text variant through Sora) — verified against the acceptance criteria and left as-is. Item 2 (branded icon/splash): assets/icon.png and assets/splash-icon.png were still the generic Expo template — a purple/indigo gradient with a white ring glyph, the exact 'AI slop gradient' the brand brief bans. Regenerated both from an SVG built from the same TONGUE_PATH and flame.dark gradient stops as components/Flame.tsx (rendered with a local sharp script, no new runtime dependency — it only ran to produce the PNGs), so the icon is the actual signature flame mark on the dark-hearth radial background, and the splash is the same mark on a transparent background so it sits on app.json's existing #14100D splash background color. Item 3 (notification denied state): lib/notifications.ts gained `getNotificationPermissionStatus()` (reads OS status without prompting) and `openSystemNotificationSettings()` (Linking.openSettings(), native-only). Settings now polls status on every focus (useFocusEffect, since the realistic path back is the user leaving for the system Settings app) and swaps the toggle row for a distinct 'Notifications are off' row with a subtitle explaining why and an 'Enable' affordance that deep-links to system settings, whenever the OS has denied permission — the plain toggle now also actually calls requestNotificationPermission() when switched on instead of just flipping a local flag blindly. Item 4 (empty states): Reflection already showed EmptyState for a brand-new account with no week yet. Home's anchor cards did not: onboarding lets a user's three picks land unevenly across anchors (e.g. all three in midday/evening), leaving an anchor with zero committed habits and a blank body under its header. Added an EmptyState ('Nothing here yet.' / 'You didn't pick a habit for this anchor — skip it and tend the other two.') for that case, plus swapped the progress ring's '0/0' label for a plain '—' so an intentionally-empty anchor doesn't read as a broken counter. Item 5 (re-engagement beat): the existing `flameJustDimmed` banner fires on any single missed day but is not a Sheet and never offers to restart anything. Added a distinct `comebackPromptDue` flag (store/habits.ts, set by reconcile() when it finds 3+ *consecutive* fully missed days — a genuinely bad week, not just a slip) that drives a one-time Sheet on Home (constants/copy.ts#COMEBACK_PROMPT, in the same off-ramp voice as the rest of the loss-aversion copy) listing the user's own first committed habit per anchor as one-tap 'restart' options; picking one marks it kept for today and dismisses the prompt. The single-day banner is suppressed while this Sheet is pending so the two don't stack. Item 6 (restore + error handling): Restore Purchases (testID paywall-restore) and the inline `danger`-caption error state under a non-blocking in-button spinner were already built to spec — but the demo checkout provider (lib/payments.ts) always resolved successfully, so the error path was structurally present but practically unreachable in the demo build. Added a 15% simulated failure rate to the demo `purchase()` call (the PaywallProvider interface and its resolved PaywallStatus shape are untouched — only the demo implementation's rejection path changed) so a reviewer can actually see 'That didn't go through. Nothing was charged — try again.' and retry to success, matching what a real payment SDK's occasional failure/cancellation looks like. `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean after every batch of edits; `expo start`/builds/exports were not run per instructions.
